title: Ethiopia Passport / Laissez-Passer / Pilgrim Travel Document Application
description: The Immigration And Citizenship Service (ICS — the current name of the historic 'Main Department for Immigration & Nationality Affairs', whose letterhead this form still carries) application form used by an Ethiopian national to apply for a Passport, a Laissez-Passer (issued only to an Ethiopian abroad who does not hold a passport and is returning from abroad), a Pilgrim travel document (for Hajj/pilgrimage travel), or a Reissue of an existing document. Opens Ethiopia's Passport vertical (3/6 — Visa and Taxes already published via et/ics/e-visa-application and et/mor/individual-tin-registration). Models the document-type selection; the applicant's own identity/description particulars including the three-part Ethiopian given/father's/grandfather's name convention, each written once in Amharic script and once in Latin transliteration (Section 1: Applicant); the applicant's actual parents' own particulars — full name, country/city of birth, nationality, and current address (Section 2: Family Information); marital status (Section 3); a Local-or-Abroad residence address, gated on whether the applicant resides in Ethiopia or abroad (Section 4: Residence Address); and who is presenting the application — the applicant, a parent, a proxy, or a guardian — plus their declaration (Section 5: Application Presented By). Filing this application is a citizen action performed in person with the Immigration And Citizenship Service; this schema does not file the application itself, and the live source is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia or its Immigration And Citizenship Service.

source.documentRef: "Application Form" (bilingual Amharic/English), Main Department for Immigration & Nationality Affairs, native (non-AcroForm) PDF, 1 page. Mirrored unauthenticated and identically across multiple Ethiopian embassy/consulate sites (this schema's own field-level citations use the Belgium embassy mirror).

verification.notes:
GOV-4264 ("GovSchema Standard Research"). Deepens Ethiopia past its first two published schemas (et/ics/e-visa-application, GOV-3313, Visa; et/mor/individual-tin-registration, GOV-4246, Taxes), opening the Passport vertical (3 of 6) via this application form, first banked as an open-backlog candidate during the GOV-4246 cycle. Ethiopia's Business Formation vertical (the Ethiopian Investment Commission's 'Application Form for New Investment Permit') was screened again this cycle as the sibling open-backlog candidate and re-confirmed a legacy OLE2 binary `.doc` (magic bytes `d0 cf 11 e0`), with no straightforward extraction path in this environment (no antiword/catdoc/python-docx/LibreOffice); this passport form was chosen instead as a genuine PDF (magic `%PDF-1.3`).

Fetched directly with a realistic desktop User-Agent from `ethiopianembassy.be`: HTTP 200, `Content-Type: application/pdf`, 108,133 bytes, sha256 `ad07ad0b94641362be887e419609b3db919e21c9272cdd80bec867677b6ad08f`, no login/CAPTCHA/Cloudflare gate. Cross-corroborated as the standard, widely-mirrored official form (not a stale or embassy-specific variant) via a web search turning up the identical form independently mirrored on Scribd and other Ethiopian embassy sites, all under the same 'Main Department for Immigration & Nationality Affairs' letterhead. The domestic online-portal variant (`passport.ics.gov.et`, the successor to the formerly-checked `ethiopianpassportservices.gov.et`) remains unreachable from this session's tooling (connection failure, no TLS handshake) despite `ics.gov.et` itself resolving and returning HTTP 200 — the same caveat the GOV-4246 cycle flagged, re-confirmed rather than newly discovered; a future cycle with different network access should re-check whether the online portal's own form composition has diverged from this printed specimen.

Extracted with `pdfjs-dist` (vendored at `/tmp/node_modules/pdfjs-dist`), reading each text item's raw string plus its `transform` x/y position to reconstruct the form's true row-by-row and column-by-column layout (the Amharic glyphs render as mojibake through this library's font substitution, but the interleaved English labels and blank-line positions are fully legible and sufficient to identify every field). The single page's 5 numbered sections were confirmed strictly top-to-bottom by descending y-coordinate of each section heading (563.2, 353.2, 251.9, 206.2, 91.3), with the document-type checkbox row (Passport / Laissez-Passer / Pilgrim / Reissue) printed above Section 1 with no section number of its own.

Four disclosed source-fidelity findings: (1) Section 1's Name/Father's Name/Grandfather's Name rows each print a bilingual pair of full-width blank lines, one Amharic-labelled and one English-labelled, at uniform ~16.5pt row spacing with no other visual distinction — modelled as two separate fields per name component (e.g. `applicantGivenName`/`applicantGivenNameLatin`) rather than one shared blank, since the form's own layout draws two independent writable lines rather than one line with a bilingual label; disclosed as an interpretation choice, not a printed instruction distinguishing the two purposes. These three name components reflect the Ethiopian patronymic naming convention (given name / father's given name / grandfather's given name), distinct from Section 2's `parentFullName` fields, which are the applicant's actual parents' own full names for genealogy/nationality-proof purposes. (2) The three previous-passport fields (`previousPassportNumber`/`previousPassportPlaceOfIssue`/`previousPassportValidUntil`, printed directly beneath the Section 1 date-of-birth/place-of-birth row) carry no printed conditional instruction of their own tying them to the top document-type checkboxes or to any other field; while they most plausibly apply to an applicant who selects 'Reissue' or already holds a passport, the form prints no explicit gate, so they are modelled bare-optional (`required: false`, no `requiredWhen`) rather than fabricating a `documentType`-based condition the form itself does not state. (3) Section 4's 'Local' and 'Abroad' column headers have no printed checkbox distinguishing which column an applicant should complete — modelled as a directly-supplied boolean gate, `residesAbroad` (not itself a printed field), the same convention this registry's Malta/Cyprus passport schemas use for their own unprinted section-applicability conditions; Local fields (`region`/`zone`/`woredaOrKKetema`/`kebele`/`houseNumber`/`localPhoneNumber`) are `requiredWhen residesAbroad equals false`, Abroad fields (`abroadCountry`/`abroadAddress`/`abroadCity`/`abroadState`/`abroadZipCode`/`abroadDayTimeTelephone`/`abroadEveningTelephone`/`abroadEmail`) `requiredWhen residesAbroad equals true`. (4) Section 3's 'IF OTHER, SPECIFY' free-text field is modelled `requiredWhen maritalStatus equals OTHER`, since the form prints only SINGLE/MARRIED as named checkboxes plus this catch-all.

Models 48 `fields[]` across 6 steps (Document Type; Section 1 Applicant; Section 2 Family Information; Section 3 Marital Status; Section 4 Residence Address; Section 5 Application Presented By) plus 1 `documents[]` attestation entry (the form's own closing declaration statement). 2 valid mock scenarios (a local-resident single first-time applicant with no previous passport; an abroad-resident married applicant whose application is presented by a proxy) plus 6 mutation-control fixtures (a missing statically-required field, a missing `ifOtherSpecify` while `maritalStatus` is `OTHER`, a missing local field while `residesAbroad` is false, a missing abroad field while `residesAbroad` is true, an invalid `sex` enum value, and an unknown top-level field) are committed under `conformance/et/ics/passport-application/1.0.0/`. An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required/requiredWhen rules directly from this schema's own `fields[]`, discarded after use, not committed) ran all 8: both valid scenarios at 0 errors, all 6 mutation controls each raising exactly 1 error, and confirmed every `requiredWhen` field reference resolves (0 dangling references). Validated clean with `node tools/validate.mjs` and `node tools/validate-ajv.mjs`, individually and as part of the full registry run.

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: Document type
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, header checkbox row — "PASSPORT / LAISSEZ-PASSER / PILGRIM / REISSUE"
fields.description: The form's own note (below this checkbox row) states Laissez-Passer is issued only to an Ethiopian abroad who does not hold a passport and is returning from abroad, that only Pilgrims may hold more than one travel document at a time, and that the form itself is to be filled by Ethiopians only.

fields.name: applicantFathersGivenName
fields.label: Father's name (Amharic)
fields.description: Ethiopian patronymic naming convention: the applicant's own full legal name is given name + father's given name + grandfather's given name, distinct from Section 2's `fatherFullName` (the applicant's actual father, as a separate person, for genealogy/nationality purposes).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 1 — "የአባት ስም" / bilingual pair with "FATHER'S NAME"

fields.name: applicantGrandfathersGivenName
fields.label: Grandfather's name (Amharic)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 1 — "የአያት ስም" / bilingual pair with "GRAND FATHER'S NAME"

fields.name: previousPassportNumber
fields.label: Previous passport number
fields.description: No printed instruction ties this field or its two neighbours to `documentType` or any other field, even though it most plausibly applies to a Reissue applicant or one renewing an existing passport — modelled bare-optional rather than fabricating an unprinted condition.

fields.name: previousPassportValidUntil
fields.label: Previous passport — valid until
fields.description: Same disclosed no-printed-signal treatment as `previousPassportNumber`. Printed as a free-text blank, not a labelled date field, so modelled as `string` rather than `date`.

fields.name: fatherFullName
fields.label: Father's full name
fields.description: The applicant's actual father, as a separate person — distinct from Section 1's `applicantFathersGivenName`, which is a component of the applicant's own name.

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital status
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 3 — "SINGLE / MARRIED / IF OTHER, SPECIFY"

fields.name: residesAbroad
fields.label: Applicant resides abroad
fields.description: Not a printed checkbox. Section 4 prints two side-by-side column headers, 'LOCAL' and 'ABROAD', with no field distinguishing which an applicant should complete — modelled as this directly-supplied boolean gate, the same convention this registry's Malta/Cyprus passport schemas use for their own unprinted section-applicability conditions.

fields.name: abroadDayTimeTelephone
fields.label: Day time telephone (abroad)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 4, Abroad column — "DAY TIME TEL."

documents.label: Presenter's Declaration
documents.statement: I, THE UNDERSIGNED DECLARE THAT THE ABOVE STATEMENTS ARE COMPLETE, TRUE AND CORRECT TO THE BEST OF MY KNOWLEDGE.
documents.sourceRef: Page 1, declaration text above Section 5's signature block
